questions.question: A game theorist is analyzing a game of chess where both players can randomize their moves. Which of the following is a key concept in this context?
questions.hint: Consider the implications of randomization on Nash Equilibrium

questions.question: A researcher is developing an algorithm for expert diagnosis in a medical imaging context. What type of strategy is likely to be employed to account for the uncertainty in diagnosis?
questions.hint: Think about the role of probability in diagnosis

questions.question: A game designer is creating a game where players can switch between two different strategies. Which concept from game theory is relevant to this scenario?
questions.hint: Consider the implications of mixed strategies on player behavior
